channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Flying tipping, unauthorised parking, 27th March
item.description:
Despite a penalty notice being issued more rubbish has been added to the vehicle today Also untaxed (as is the white car, there the other day, adding to the rubbish) So probably no insurance either. Hope this vehicle is moved asap
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Restons Crescent, Avery Hill
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Still not working, 27th March
item.description:
Countdown not working. Reported several times. Also countdown not working at the next bus stop outside MIND.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 49, Beckenham Road, Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipped very large item that looks like an Agra /cooker, 27th March
item.description:
Just watched the family from 10 Oslo square fly tipping. Yet again, this family continue to litter our green areas with their rubbish. Why do neighbours have to constantly put up with looking at their rubbish instead of the family disposing of it all in the correct manner. They have dustbins, a very large car and at least 6 adults living at the property that could help to remove items of rubbish in the correct manner. Maybe the council should fine them!
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Plover Way, Rotherhithe, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Yet more flytipping, 27th March
item.description:
Many bags of both recycling and garbage placed incorrectly in recycling bags have been left in this location for days. Not by a residence. Purposefully dumped.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Wilderton Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Chicken shop fly tipping again, 27th March
item.description:
Different week, same chicken shop dumping their rubbish

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Worsley Bridge Road, New Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti, 27th March
item.description:
Email received by CSC inbox 27/03/26 11:17 Graffiti - Non offensive Lat/Long - 51.4315628, 0.0377065 what3words - polite.funny.drips Nearest address - 85 Lambscroft Avenue, Grove Park, London, SE9 4PD Mottingham Recreation Ground. Remove graffiti from cycle barriers at entrance nearest Kingsfield House Reference - 5442985 Map link - open Google map Report history - Status: Open 27/03/2026 11:17 Mottingham Recreation Ground. Remove graffiti from cycle barriers at entrance nearest Kingsfield House
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lambscroft Avenue, Chinbrook, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Anti social parking, 27th March
item.description:
Repeated parking of heavy vehicles is damaging the character of the residential street and minimising sightlines for other drivers

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 22, Trinity Street, The Borough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Outside number 10, 27th March
item.description:
Broken tree branch
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Richborough Road, West Hampstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: On 2nd floor shared Terrace of Jigger mast house, mast quay SE18 5NH. Someone threw a plastic bag of shit, 27th March
item.description:
Reported 4 days before to concierge and management. Concierge people said they can’t share management name and details. It’s flies all around the terrace.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Mast Quay, Woolwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti again on the Tesco on Old Woolwich Road, 27th March
item.description:
Great if you can paint over this again

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Trafalgar Road, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Broken Bench, 27th March
item.description:
This bench has been broken for a long time. Its dangerous. It is next to the bus stop outside block of flats 90/100 Little Heath se78bh

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Little Heath, Charlton, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Cardboard boxes blowing into road, 27th March
item.description:
Collapsed cardboard boxes are overflowing from the bins on the forecourt of the Londis shop (St Nicholas Food & Wine). The boxes are being blown by the wind out onto the busy road and now pose a threat to motorists. Not the first time this has happened.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Plumstead Common Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Black bag of rubbish on the corner of King William Lane and Old Woolwich Road, 27th March
item.description:
Great if you can pick this up

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): King William Lane, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Outside 119 Clerkenwell Road Ec1r 5dq, 27th March
item.description:
Manhole is rocking noisily. It seems like it's very loose because push bikes make it rock too. Thanks

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Clerkenwell Road, Holborn, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Food waste, 27th March
item.description:
Email received by CSC inbox 27/03/26 10:08 • Please give brief details of your complaint or compliment below: There has been hazardious food waste dumped near the bin area of ravenleight court 89 bromley common. • What action (if any) would you like us to take? Remive the waste
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 101, Bromley Common, Bromley Common, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: My street is being used as a skip., 27th March
item.description:
Road has been used as a skip for works being carried out by SGN in neighboring roads , road is a complete mess and now having to pay from a permit to park on my own road to not even be able to park near my house

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Tunstock Way, Upper Belvedere, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bottle smashed on street, 27th March
item.description:
Bottle smashed on street

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Tunnel Avenue, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Builders rubbish dumped, 27th March
item.description:
It would appear that some builder has dumped his waste

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Page Heath Villas, Widmore, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped chest of drawers, 27th March
item.description:
Against school wall in Kingsdale Road a dumped old small chest of drawers on the pavement
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Kingsdale Road, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dangerous pothole outside of nearby flats, 27th March
item.description:
This is a major pothole that seems to be a reoccurring problem on the very same road and it's located off the street road bullace row it's exact location is just outside of lamb house on the edge of the estate parking and this pothole is a dangerous one it's very deep and can do some serious damage to cars and even anyone who could accidentally catch there foot in it and to make matters worse it's been fixed many times before but gets cheaply filled in with tarmac then several months later the whole reappears due to easy wear and tear the entire road itself is badly worn out if anything I would personally say the whole road needs completely redoing it's very bad and like I said before this is a seriously dangerous pothole it's even worse when it fills up due to rain like a giant puddle with no idea of how deep it could be when it's filled up looks worse then when it's dry

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 252, Camberwell Road, Camberwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
